config.rationale: Experiment 4/30 — directly addresses the user's second request: quantify the speedup from vLLM's --language-model-only flag on Qwen3.5-9B (a VLM with has_mtp=true). Holds ALL other variables identical to the successful vLLM baseline 8e036532f8f1: TP=1, max_model_len=65536, gpu_memory_utilization=0.9, dtype=auto, kv_cache_dtype=auto, no chunked prefill, no prefix caching, enforce_eager=false, fcfs. The ONLY delta is adding --language-model-only via extra_engine_args, so any change vs baseline (7098.5 tok/s, TTFT p95 45.6 ms, TPOT p95 10.38 ms) is purely attributable to skipping the vision tower load + multimodal preprocessing path. Expected effect: lower memory footprint (vision tower freed → more KV cache), faster startup, and slight throughput uplift on text-only workloads. I deliberately did NOT layer in chunked prefill, prefix caching, or quantization in this run — that would muddle the LM-only attribution the user explicitly asked for. Following Rule 11: not repeating the failed SGLang NEXTN config (7463cc35c921 healthcheck_timeout); will revisit MTP later via a different SGLang invocation (e.g., without SGLANG_ENABLE_SPEC_V2 env, or different speculative-num-draft-tokens) once we've isolated the language-model-only effect on vLLM. Memory: 7.46B bf16 ≈ 15 GB; with vision tower skipped probably ~13 GB → ~67 GB free for KV at 0.9 util — plenty for 65k context. No OOM risk.

smoke_tests.json_mode_detail: No content in response (reasoning model may not support JSON mode)

llm_commentary: Adding --language-model-only delivered a marginal TTFT p95 improvement (44.5ms vs 45.6ms baseline, ~2.4% better) and now holds the latency crown, but throughput dipped slightly to 7048.6 tok/s (~0.7% below the 7098.5 baseline), so it does not displace the throughput or balanced leader. The flag's expected memory savings did not translate into a meaningful throughput uplift at default KV-cache settings, suggesting we're not KV-bound at this concurrency. Smoke tests pass for chat/tool calling/JSON schema (JSON mode failure is a known reasoning-model quirk, not config-related). The two experiments now form a tight Pareto front; further gains likely require pushing batching/KV knobs or trying chunked prefill.
docker_command: docker run --name bench-vllm-6c6509c4d045 --gpus all --shm-size 16g --network host -v /root/.cache/huggingface:/root/.cache/huggingface -d vllm/vllm-openai:latest --model Qwen/Qwen3.5-9B --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000 --tensor-parallel-size 1 --dtype auto --gpu-memory-utilization 0.9 --max-model-len 65536 --language-model-only --enable-auto-tool-choice --tool-call-parser qwen3_coder --reasoning-parser qwen3
